known_issues.title: AFM lifter failure / valve train tick
known_issues.description: The Active Fuel Management system on the 5.3L and 6.2L EcoTec3 V8s deactivates cylinders to save fuel, and the lifters responsible for this can collapse, producing a persistent tick and eventually camshaft damage.
known_issues.symptoms: A persistent ticking or tapping noise from the engine at idle that becomes more pronounced when the engine switches between 8-cylinder and 4-cylinder mode.
known_issues.recommendation: Listen closely for a ticking noise at idle, especially during the transition between 8-cylinder and 4-cylinder mode, and ask about AFM disabler installation.

known_issues.title: HVAC blend door actuator failure
known_issues.description: The blend door actuator behind the dash commonly fails, causing a repetitive clicking noise and inconsistent cabin temperature.
known_issues.symptoms: A repetitive clicking noise from behind the dashboard and cabin temperature that doesn't match the climate control setting.
known_issues.recommendation: Run the climate control through its full range and listen for clicking behind the dash.

known_issues.title: 6L80 transmission shudder and rough shifts
known_issues.description: The 6-speed automatic can develop a shudder in torque converter lockup or harsh 1-2 shifts as fluid and clutch packs age.
known_issues.symptoms: A shudder felt through the floor at steady highway cruise, or an abrupt clunk when shifting from 1st to 2nd gear.
known_issues.recommendation: Test drive at steady highway speed and light throttle to feel for shudder, and ask for transmission fluid service records.

known_issues.title: Power steering pump and rack leaks
known_issues.description: Power steering components can develop leaks over time, leading to fluid loss and increased steering effort.
known_issues.symptoms: Increasingly heavy steering effort, a whining noise when turning, and visible fluid residue underneath the front of the truck.
known_issues.recommendation: Check under the front of the truck for fluid residue and feel for steering assist consistency.

known_issues.title: Rear differential whine
known_issues.description: The rear differential can develop a whine or howl as gears wear, particularly on trucks used for towing.
known_issues.symptoms: A whining or howling noise from underneath the truck that changes pitch with vehicle speed, most noticeable while decelerating.
known_issues.recommendation: Listen for whining noise that changes with speed during the test drive, especially decelerating.
